title: Converting a small number to scientific notation

steps.instruction: Move the decimal point right until the number is between 1 and 10.
steps.working: Moving the decimal point 4 places to the right: $0.000307 \to 3.07$

steps.instruction: Determine the power of 10. Since the original number is less than 1, the exponent is negative. The number of places moved gives the magnitude of the exponent.
steps.working: Moved 4 places to the right $\Rightarrow$ exponent is $-4$

steps.instruction: Write the number in scientific notation and verify.

title: Multiplying numbers in scientific notation
problem: Calculate $(6.2 \times 10^5) \times (3 \times 10^{-2})$ and express the answer in scientific notation.

steps.instruction: Multiply the powers of 10 by adding the exponents.
Adjust so the coefficient is between 1 and 10.

title: Solving a real-world problem using scientific notation
problem: A spacecraft travels at $2.4 \times 10^4$ km/h. It needs to cover a distance of $1.8 \times 10^9$ km to reach a space station. How many hours will the journey take? Express the answer in scientific notation.

steps.instruction: Divide the powers of 10 by subtracting the exponents.
Combine and adjust so the coefficient is between 1 and 10.
